title: ristantia pachysperma
intro: Ristantia pachysperma, also known as the "tree with yellow flowers", is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical forests of Australia and the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ristantia pachysperma can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
